findings.message: Target PR #4 changes exactly the eight approved Social Media starter app files and no extra files.
evidence: GitHub changed-file list returned README.md, index.html, package.json, src/data/templates.js, src/lib/safety.js, src/lib/status.js, src/main.js, and src/styles.css.
Target PR head SHA 02a749ff3348be9c57f34726f104f381e228b1cd matches the recorded build execution.

findings.message: The content sprint includes the requested 7-day calendar, 21 draft post packages, one weekly report draft, platform targeting, hooks, captions, evidence notes, risk notes, and pending approval state.
evidence: src/data/templates.js exports FIRST_CONTENT_SPRINT with calendarDays 7, totalDraftPostPackages 21, weeklyReportDrafts 1, and 21 package records.

Each package includes day, platformTarget, contentPillar, hook, captionDraft, shortFormVideoIdea, carouselStoryAngle, cta, approvalStatus pending, livePostingBlocked true, sourceEvidenceNote, and riskNote.
src/main.js renders the content sprint, daily calendar summary, post package cards, weekly report draft, pending approvals, and blocked live actions.

findings.message: Mandatory live-action blocks are preserved across accounts, sprint defaults, UI, and safety assertions.
evidence: Every platform remains handle not_provided, handleStatus pending_owner_input, connectionStatus not_connected, postingMode draft_only, approvalRequired true, livePostingBlocked true, and credentialsStored false.

src/lib/safety.js rejects missing sprint data, wrong package counts, non-pending approval state, unblocked posting, scheduling, analytics API, social OAuth, credentials, n8n automation, and packages without evidence or risk notes.

README.md documents no social OAuth, credentials, posting, scheduling, DMs/comments, analytics API, n8n activation, paid tools, production deploy, unverified results, invented revenue, or fabricated case studies.

findings.message: The draft content avoids unverified client outcomes by using source-controlled AG OS proof context or educational/build-in-public framing.
evidence: Draft posts reference AG OS proof chains, inactive draft workflow proof, staging proof records, Bootstrap Mode, Cost OS, and dashboard/control center context without claiming customer revenue or unrecorded case studies.
Risk notes explicitly block customer outcome, revenue, live workflow, live social, and unproven case-study claims.

findings.message: Safe local target checks passed without dependency installation, live connector calls, deployment, or external services.
evidence: npm.cmd run check passed in the target branch checkout.
npm.cmd test passed in the target branch checkout.

Direct draft-only safety assertion returned safetyValid true, 21 packages, seven days, one weekly report draft, 21 pending packages, livePostingBlocked true, credentialsStored false, and socialOauthConnected false.
git diff --check passed.

findings.message: The target PR is ready for owner-approved merge after these review records are merged, but this critique cannot approve or perform the merge.
evidence: GitHub reports target PR #4 is open, unmerged, and mergeable.
Critique authority is advisory_only and blocksBuildMode is false.
A separate target PR merge action remains required.

optionalImprovements: Run a browser responsive and accessibility review before broader external use.
Keep platform handle entry as owner-controlled input before any future account connection approval.
After target PR merge, redeploy only the dedicated Netlify staging-only site and record the deploy result.
Before future live social work, create separate approval packages for OAuth, scheduling, posting, analytics, and n8n activation.
